heading: Non-Federal share of project costs
label: 200506 Non-Federal share of project costs
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)200506.16 U.S.C. 2508, , .Pub. L. 95–625, title X, § 100992 Stat. 3543Nov. 10, 1978In subsection (a), the word “recovery” is added after “Reasonable local costs of”, and the words “a recovery action program grant” are substituted for “program development grants”, for clarity. L. 95–625, title X, § 100992 Stat. 3543Nov. 10, 1978In subsection (a), the word “recovery” is added after “Reasonable local costs of”, and the words “a recovery action program grant” are substituted for “program development grants”, for clarity. children.heading: .—Allowable sources
children.label: 1 .—Allowable sources
children.text: The non-Federal share of project costs assisted under this chapter may be derived from general or special purpose State or local revenues, State categorical grants, special appropriations by State legislatures, donations of land, buildings, or building materials, and in-kind construction, technical, and planning services. Reasonable local costs of recovery action program development to meet the requirements of may be used as part of the local match only when the local government has not received a recovery action program grant.section 200504(a) of this title

children.heading: .—Non-allowable sources
children.label: 2 .—Non-allowable sources
children.text: No amount from the Land and Water Conservation Fund or from any other Federal grant program other than the community development block grant programs shall be used to match Federal grants under this program.

children.heading: .—Encouragement of States and Private Interests
children.label: b .—Encouragement of States and Private Interests
children.text: The Secretary shall encourage States and private interests to contribute, to the maximum extent possible, to the non-Federal share of project costs.
